subject: Mathematics
title: Twin Primes
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: Twin Primes.

hook.text: Sometimes primes appear in pairs just two apart, like 11 and 13 or 29 and 31.

buildup.text: These are called twin primes. They show up throughout the number line.

discovery.text: Mathematicians believe twin primes never stop, but nobody has proven it yet.

twist.text: In 2013, Yitang Zhang shocked the world by proving a weaker version after years of obscurity.

climax.text: His work showed that infinitely many prime pairs exist within some bounded gap.

punchline.text: Primes love company, but they won't prove it.

quiz.question: What are twin primes?
options: Two primes that multiply to give a third prime
